title: Performing an Action or Experiencing (After & Past)

senses.definition: to find out how someone is doing, especially if one has not seen or heard from them for a while

examples.x: When I ran into Sarah at the store, she asked after you and wanted to know if you were feeling better.
When I saw her at the party, I asked after your sister and how she's been feeling since her surgery.
Please pass on my regards to John and ask after his family; I heard they've been going through a tough time.
I ran into your neighbor, and he asked after you and your kids, making sure everything is alright.
Don't forget to ask after our elderly relatives when you visit them in the nursing home, and see if they need anything.

senses.definition: to follow or chase someone, often with the intent of catching or reaching them

examples.x: The dog escaped from the backyard, and the children came after it, trying to bring it back home.
The paparazzi relentlessly came after the celebrity, hoping to capture exclusive photos.
The detectives came after the suspect with all the evidence they had gathered.
The guards came after the thieves who had escaped from the prison.
The debt collectors came after him for the unpaid bills, making his financial situation even more stressful.

senses.definition: to take action or make an effort to pursue or attain something

examples.x: He's spending too much time on social media instead of getting after his work responsibilities.
When opportunities arise, he doesn't hesitate to get after them.
She always gets after her passions, no matter the obstacles.
In pursuit of his dream job, he's ready to get after it with unwavering dedication.
The team decided to get after the championship and put in their best effort.

senses.definition: to pursue or try to catch someone or something

examples.x: The detective decided to go after the suspect, who was fleeing the crime scene.
The police officer had to go after the speeding car to pull it over for a traffic violation.
The cat's instinct was to go after the bird perched on the windowsill.
He had to go after the lost kite that had been carried away by the wind.
They went after the runaway dog, which had escaped into the neighborhood.

senses.definition: to take care of someone or something and attend to their needs, well-being, or safety

examples.x: The nurse looks after the sick patient by monitoring their condition and providing medication.
My cat looks after herself very well.
The gardener looks after the garden by weeding, watering, and pruning the plants.
My grandmother looked after me after my parents passed away.
The company looks after its employees by providing them with a safe and healthy work environment.

senses.definition: to go after someone or something in order to catch them

examples.x: I'm going to make after the train.
The detective made after the suspect who had just robbed the bank.
The dog made after the cat, but the cat was able to climb up a tree.
The children made after the ball, but it rolled into the street.
The man made after the thief, but he was able to disappear into the crowd.

senses.definition: to give someone or something a name in honor or in memory of another person or thing

examples.x: The street was named after a prominent community leader.
The street was named after a local war hero.

senses.definition: to follow someone or something in an attempt to catch them

examples.x: The police had to run after the suspect to apprehend him.
The children love to run after the ice cream truck when it comes through the neighborhood.
He's always eager to run after his dreams of becoming a successful musician.
We decided to run after the thief who snatched the purse.
She always loved to run after butterflies in the garden during summer.

senses.definition: to look or act like an older member of the family, especially one's parents

examples.x: The little girl strongly takes after her mother.
The baby takes after his father with those adorable dimples.
Both sisters take after their mother in terms of kindness.
The family members take after each other in their distinctive smiles.
The teenager takes after his older brother in fashion sense.

senses.definition: to move past someone with speed

examples.x: The fast runner blew past others in the race effortlessly.
The company's profits blew past the projected figures for the quarter.
The ambitious project is set to blow past the initial timeline.
The talented musician continues to blow past expectations with each new release.

senses.definition: to move beyond a specific location, object, or person

examples.x: We'll need to go past the store to reach the park.
The bus will go past the school before reaching the bus stop.
They had to go past the security checkpoint at the airport.
If you go past the bridge, you've gone too far.

senses.definition: to present an idea or proposal to someone with the intention of getting their opinion or approval

examples.x: I need to run past my new project idea with the team before I proceed.
